videos.title: Growing Patterns | How Patterns Grow Step by Step
videos.description: Understand how growing patterns work. See how each step adds more and learn the rule behind growing patterns with shapes and numbers.
learningObjectives: Understand the difference between repeating and growing patterns
Identify how each step in a growing pattern adds more
keyConceptsCovered: Growing patterns increase or decrease at each step
Growing patterns are different from repeating patterns

videos.title: Growing Patterns with Shapes | Building Staircase Patterns
videos.description: Watch growing patterns come to life with blocks and shapes. Build staircase patterns, pyramid patterns, and see how each step adds more than the last.
learningObjectives: Build growing patterns with concrete objects
Count items at each step to find the growth rule
keyConceptsCovered: Staircase pattern: 1, 2, 3, 4 blocks at each step
Pyramid pattern: adds one more row each time
Counting items at each step reveals the rule

videos.title: Growing Patterns in Nature | Spirals, Flowers, and Trees
videos.description: Discover how growing patterns appear in nature! See spirals in seashells, petals on flowers, and branches on trees. Nature is full of growing patterns.
learningObjectives: Recognize growing patterns in the natural world
keyConceptsCovered: Spiral shells follow a growing pattern
Flower petals often follow number patterns

videos.title: Growing Pattern Problems | Draw the Next Step
videos.description: Practice growing patterns by drawing what comes next. Each problem shows a pattern with steps, and you need to figure out the rule and draw the next step.
learningObjectives: Extend growing patterns by drawing the next step
Explain the growth rule in words
keyConceptsCovered: Identifying the amount added at each step
Verifying the rule works for all steps

videos.title: Repeating vs Growing Patterns | Can You Tell the Difference?
videos.description: Challenge yourself! Look at different patterns and decide: is it repeating or growing? A fun comparison activity that tests your understanding of both pattern types.
learningObjectives: Distinguish between repeating and growing patterns
Explain why a pattern is repeating or growing
keyConceptsCovered: Repeating = same group over and over
Growing = each step adds or changes

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Fast-track through growing patterns with nature application and comparison challenge

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of growing patterns with practice and challenge

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Core growing pattern concepts after prerequisite review

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: A staircase pattern has 1 block in step 1, 2 blocks in step 2, 3 blocks in step 3. How many blocks in step 5?
questions.explanation: The pattern adds 1 block at each step. Step 4 has 4 blocks and step 5 has 5 blocks.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Use building blocks or stacking cubes to create growing patterns physically. Have students count items at each step and record in a table. Videos 1-3 are essential for all students.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Help your child build growing patterns with household items: stack coins (1, 2, 3, 4), arrange buttons in growing rows. Ask 'How many more did we add each time?'
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Try building your own growing patterns with blocks or coins. Count how many you have at each step. Can you guess how many you will need for the next step?
